whatThisIs: Look up renal dose adjustments for commonly prescribed antibiotics. Sophie Well covers the agents whose FDA labels include explicit CrCl- or eGFR-based dose-reduction tables - vancomycin, piperacillin-tazobactam, cefepime, meropenem, levofloxacin, ciprofloxacin, trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole, gabapentin (frequently bundled with antibiotic regimens), and the rest of the common renal-adjusted list. Enter a patient's CrCl or eGFR and the agent, and the tool returns the dose, the interval, and the threshold the label crossed to get there.
whenToUse: Use this when a patient with reduced kidney function is starting empiric or targeted antibiotics, when reviewing whether a standing order should be tapered after a creatinine bump, or when the pharmacy is verifying an order against the package insert. The dataset is a subset of FDA labels via DailyMed - it is not comprehensive across every dialysis modality or every off-label scenario - so the table is a starting point and pharmacy review remains the final check for any non-trivial renal regimen.
